commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces Telegram global text/media buffer flush chains with per-key queues, scopes media groups by chat/thread/media_group_id, and adds per-group outbound lane fairness plus regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection. Current main uses process-wide buffered flush chains and a media_group_id-only media buffer key, and the PR supplies before/after focused tests that fail on main and pass on the branch; I did not run tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is needed; the remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer live-proof review for the existing draft PR.

target.title: Parallelize Telegram topic buffers and outbound fairness
target.body:
## Summary
- Replace Telegram text-fragment and media-group global flush chains with per-buffer-key queues so independent forum topics can flush at the same time.
- Scope media group buffering by chat + thread + Telegram media_group_id so identical album ids in different topics cannot merge or block each other.
- Add a per-group fair outbound queue ahead of the existing grammY throttler so one topic/message lane cannot monopolize Telegram's shared group send pipe.
- Add regression coverage for both handler-level topic buffer parallelism and outbound throttler lane fairness.

## Root Cause
Telegram long-text fragment flushing and media-group flushing each used one process-wide promise chain. That preserved ordering, but it serialized unrelated topics. Media groups also used only Telegram's `media_group_id` as the buffer key, so two topics with the same album id could share one buffer lane.

After those ingress paths were parallelized, Telegram-visible output could still feel serialized because the account API throttler feeds all group sends for the same `chat_id` into grammY's group limiter. Telegram forum topics share that `chat_id`, so bursts of progress/edit traffic from one topic could sit ahead of another topic's outbound update even though the agent work was already running in parallel.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Telegram forum topics no longer block each other's buffered text-fragment/media-group flushes, and same-group outbound API requests are fair-shared by topic/message lane before entering Telegram's rate-limit-preserving throttler.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw Telegram e2e harness and focused Telegram account-throttler tests in a temp worktree, exercising supergroup/forum `message_thread_id` payloads and group API payload ordering.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_E2E_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.media.stickers-and-fragments.e2e.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.media.downloads-media-file-path-no-file-download.e2e.test.ts --testNamePattern 'flushes (different forum topic fragments|same-id forum topic media groups) in parallel'`

Evidence after fix: `Test Files 2 passed (2); Tests 2 passed | 10 skipped (12)`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/account-throttler.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: `Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 3 passed (3)`.

Observed result after fix: handler tests reached two reply starts while the first topic's reply was still held open; throttler tests admitted the other topic/message lane before the same lane's second queued request.

What was not tested: live Telegram Desktop/manual transport proof was not rerun for this update; these are the closest deterministic Telegram handler and outbound scheduler proofs.

Before proof: applied only the new handler tests to unpatched `origin/main` and ran the same filtered e2e command. Result: `Test Files 2 failed (2); Tests 2 failed | 10 skipped (12)`, with both failures reporting `expected "vi.fn()" to be called 2 times, but got 1 times`.

## Verification
- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `git diff --check`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `OPENCLAW_E2E_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.media.stickers-and-fragments.e2e.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.media.downloads-media-file-path-no-file-download.e2e.test.ts`
  - `Test Files 2 passed (2); Tests 12 passed (12)`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/account-throttler.test.ts`
  - `Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 3 passed (3)`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --reviewer codex --fallback-reviewer none`
  - `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`

## What was not tested
- Full repository test suite beyond autoreview's broad check path.
- Live Telegram account proof for the outbound fairness update.
